The valley extends into [California](/wiki/California "California")'s [San Bernardino County](/wiki/San_Bernardino_County,_California "San Bernardino County, California"); the northern side of the valley extends into extreme southeast [Clark County](/wiki/Clark_County,_Nevada "Clark County, Nevada"), [Nevada](/wiki/Nevada "Nevada"). The main part of the valley lies in southwest [Mohave County](/wiki/Mohave_County,_Arizona "Mohave County, Arizona"), Arizona and is at the intersection of the southeast [Mojave](/wiki/Mojave_Desert "Mojave Desert") and northwest [Sonoran](/wiki/Sonoran_Desert "Sonoran Desert") deserts.

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [34°57′25″N 114°35′5″W﻿ / ﻿34.95694°N 114.58472°W﻿ / 34.95694; -114.58472](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Mohave_Valley&params=34_57_25_N_114_35_5_W_type:river)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Landform along the Colorado River in Arizona

For the Arizona city in the Mohave Valley, Lower Colorado River, see [Mohave Valley, Arizona](/wiki/Mohave_Valley,_Arizona "Mohave Valley, Arizona").

| Width | 10 mi (16 km) |
| Geography | |

| Borders on | [Colorado River](/wiki/Colorado_River "Colorado River")-W [Black Mesa (western Arizona)](/wiki/Black_Mesa_(western_Arizona) "Black Mesa (western Arizona)")-E [Black Mountains (Arizona)](/wiki/Black_Mountains_(Arizona) "Black Mountains (Arizona)")-NE [Mohave Mountains](/wiki/Mohave_Mountains "Mohave Mountains")-S [Newberry Mountains (Nevada)](/wiki/Newberry_Mountains_(Nevada) "Newberry Mountains (Nevada)")-NW [Dead Mountains](/wiki/Dead_Mountains "Dead Mountains")-W & NW [Sacramento Mountains (California)](/wiki/Sacramento_Mountains_(California) "Sacramento Mountains (California)")-SW |
| [Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system") | [34°57′25″N 114°35′5″W﻿ / ﻿34.95694°N 114.58472°W﻿ / 34.95694; -114.58472](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Mohave_Valley&params=34_57_25_N_114_35_5_W_type:river) |

The **Mohave Valley** is a [valley](/wiki/Valley "Valley") located mostly on the east shore of the south-flowing [Colorado River](/wiki/Colorado_River "Colorado River") in northwest [Arizona](/wiki/Arizona "Arizona"). The valley extends into [California](/wiki/California "California")'s [San Bernardino County](/wiki/San_Bernardino_County,_California "San Bernardino County, California"); the northern side of the valley extends into extreme southeast [Clark County](/wiki/Clark_County,_Nevada "Clark County, Nevada"), [Nevada](/wiki/Nevada "Nevada"). The main part of the valley lies in southwest [Mohave County](/wiki/Mohave_County,_Arizona "Mohave County, Arizona"), Arizona and is at the intersection of the southeast [Mojave](/wiki/Mojave_Desert "Mojave Desert") and northwest [Sonoran](/wiki/Sonoran_Desert "Sonoran Desert") deserts.

The valley extends into the three states of California, Arizona and Nevada, and the [Fort Mojave Indian Reservation](/wiki/Fort_Mojave_Indian_Reservation "Fort Mojave Indian Reservation") extends into them as well. On the west, the valley borders the [Dead Mountains](/wiki/Dead_Mountains "Dead Mountains") of California which overlook the tri-state intersection point.

[Needles](/wiki/Needles,_California "Needles, California") on [Interstate 40](/wiki/Interstate_40 "Interstate 40") lies on the southwest margin of the valley overlooking the [Havasu National Wildlife Refuge](/wiki/Havasu_National_Wildlife_Refuge "Havasu National Wildlife Refuge") and [Topock Marsh](/wiki/Topock_Marsh "Topock Marsh"). The village of [Topock](/wiki/Topock "Topock") is located at the south end of the valley where the [Chemehuevi Mountains](/wiki/Chemehuevi_Mountains "Chemehuevi Mountains") of California restrict the Colorado to the narrow [Mohave Canyon](/wiki/Mohave_Canyon "Mohave Canyon").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Mohave Valley

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mohave_Valley&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Mohave Valley")]

Mohave Valley is a 25-mile (40 km) long, north-south trending [valley](/wiki/Valley "Valley") and up to 10 miles (16 km) wide at some areas. [Mohave Valley, Arizona](/wiki/Mohave_Valley,_Arizona "Mohave Valley, Arizona"), in the center of the reservation, is at the center point of the valley. [Bullhead City](/wiki/Bullhead_City,_Arizona "Bullhead City, Arizona") is at the northern perimeter of the valley; [Needles](/wiki/Needles,_California "Needles, California") is on the southwest border of the valley in California. Downstream, and southeast, near the southern valley perimeter is the [Topock Marsh](/wiki/Topock_Marsh "Topock Marsh") in Arizona, and also on the southern perimeter, [Interstate 40 in Arizona](/wiki/Interstate_40_in_Arizona "Interstate 40 in Arizona") as it ascends uphill out of the [Sacramento Wash](/wiki/Sacramento_Wash "Sacramento Wash"), and eventually enters the entire north-south [Sacramento Valley](/wiki/Sacramento_Valley_(Arizona) "Sacramento Valley (Arizona)") which lies on the east, southeast, and partial south of the [Black Mountains](/wiki/Black_Mountains_(Arizona) "Black Mountains (Arizona)").

*Mohave Valley, Arizona*, town center of the reservation, is located at [34°57′25″N 114°35′5″W﻿ / ﻿34.95694°N 114.58472°W﻿ / 34.95694; -114.58472](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Mohave_Valley&params=34_57_25_N_114_35_5_W_type:city); the town center is at the valleys center, and slightly west, closer to the Colorado River, the valley's west perimeter in many parts. The [Boundary Cone](/wiki/Boundary_Cone "Boundary Cone") landmark, lies east, at the northwest perimeter of the [Black Mesa (western Arizona)](/wiki/Black_Mesa_(western_Arizona) "Black Mesa (western Arizona)"), a subsection mountain region at the southern end of the [Black Mountains](/wiki/Black_Mountains_(Arizona) "Black Mountains (Arizona)").

## Water and watersheds

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mohave_Valley&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Water and watersheds")]

### Arizona, southeast & east-(Sacramento Wash & Valley)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mohave_Valley&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Arizona, southeast & east-(Sacramento Wash & Valley)")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b3/Needles_California_from_southwest_1.jpg/250px-Needles_California_from_southwest_1.jpg)](/wiki/File:Needles_California_from_southwest_1.jpg)

View east-northeast of Needles, Mohave Valley, & [Black Mountains (Arizona)](/wiki/Black_Mountains_(Arizona) "Black Mountains (Arizona)")

The southern end of the valley begins elevated regions, with the [Mohave Mountains](/wiki/Mohave_Mountains "Mohave Mountains") directly south, and [Dutch Flat](/wiki/Dutch_Flat_(Arizona) "Dutch Flat (Arizona)"), east of the Mohave Mountains, which drains northwest into [Sacramento Wash](/wiki/Sacramento_Wash "Sacramento Wash"). The southeast of the valley meets the outfall of Sacramento Wash, and associated drainage plains; Sacramento Valley itself is mostly east and south of the [Black Mesa](/wiki/Black_Mesa_(western_Arizona) "Black Mesa (western Arizona)") section at the south of the [Black Mountains (Arizona)](/wiki/Black_Mountains_(Arizona) "Black Mountains (Arizona)"); the Sacramento Wash turns due west, and follows [Interstate 40 in Arizona](/wiki/Interstate_40_in_Arizona "Interstate 40 in Arizona") as it turns west and parallels the wash to meet Needles at the Colorado River; it is also the southern drainage outfall point into the [Topock Marsh](/wiki/Topock_Marsh "Topock Marsh"), at [Topock](/wiki/Topock,_Arizona "Topock, Arizona"), eastern shore of the Colorado River, in the southeast valley. Other washes drain through the eastern Mohave Valley plains and foothills into the Topock Marsh from the southwest of the Black Mountains, the extensive range which goes north 75 miles (121 km) to [Lake Mead](/wiki/Lake_Mead "Lake Mead").

### Nevada, Piute Wash & Valley-(northwest)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mohave_Valley&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Nevada, Piute Wash & Valley-(northwest)")]

While in Arizona the [Sacramento Wash](/wiki/Sacramento_Wash "Sacramento Wash") drains the [Sacramento Valley](/wiki/Sacramento_Valley_(Arizona) "Sacramento Valley (Arizona)") southwest of [Kingman](/wiki/Kingman,_Arizona "Kingman, Arizona"), in Nevada the [Piute Wash](/wiki/Piute_Wash "Piute Wash") drains the [Piute Valley](/wiki/Piute_Valley "Piute Valley") of southeast Nevada. A separate drainage lies between them on the Colorado River, the *Havasu-Mohave Lakes Drainage*.[[2]](#cite_note-2) These three drainages, the west, center, and east all drain into the Mohave Valley region.
